entries.description: Authored docs/session-sets/044-ai-chat-log-discovery-and-experiments/harvest-objectives-and-redaction.md Part 1: locked the harvest-objectives matrix as 15 numbered rows across four groupings (A1-A5 identity, B1-B5 action, C1-C5 boundary, plus a D §D summary stating the minimum-viable conflict-detection triple as timestamp + engine+provider + set/session id). Each row carries an S1-hypothesis verdict on whether the signal is native, narration-required, or implicit. Out-of-scope carve-outs listed (token counts, latency, cost-per-turn already in router metrics; embedding vectors; model-internal state; prompt content per the redaction policy). Refinement window: S2 may add Claude-specific objectives; new objectives past S2 require a spec addendum.

Authored Part 2 of harvest-objectives-and-redaction.md: two-tier storage (scratch outside repo at C:\tmp\dabbler-log-harvest\, synthesis layer inside repo). Locked the synthetic-test-material rule (all harvest runs operate against a synthetic session set at C:\tmp\dabbler-log-harvest\synthetic-set\, not real Dabbler work). Locked 7 scrub rules covering verbatim user prompts (paraphrase), verbatim assistant turns (paraphrase except synthetic-set test prompts), file contents read by the assistant (elide), bearer tokens / API keys (elide), operator-local paths (substitute ~ for C:\Users\denmi\), non-public repo names (placeholder), and personally-identifying third-party content. Reading-side discipline: prefer wc / head -c / jq keys / sqlite .schema operations that surface structure without dumping content; never `cat` an entire log file. Auditability: every harvested file referenced in a discovery note must list scratch-path origin + byte count at harvest time + one-line schema description. Bundled: .gitignore extended to exclude dabbler-log-harvest/ at the repo root as belt-and-suspenders against accidental symlink relocation.

Authored discovery-notes-copilot.md §1 (artifact inventory matrix), §2 (full session-store.db schema with DDL captured verbatim from sqlite_master.sql, column names only — no row values), §3 (OTel JSONL exporter contract per `copilot help monitoring`), §5 (authentication-bridge security note flagging ~/.copilot/ide/*.lock token presence). Per Part 3 methodology of the policy doc: each artifact row populates 8 columns (Artifact / Format / Trigger / Per-process? / Schema / Surfaces signals / Redaction risk / Sample size at probe). 9 artifact rows covering config.json (JSONC), logs/process-*.log (plain text ISO-timestamped, 26 lines on the operator's instance), session-state/<uuid>/{workspace.yaml, checkpoints/index.md, files/, research/}, session-store.db (118784 bytes, 0 rows in all data tables — schema-derived findings only), ide/*.lock (CRITICAL redaction risk: plaintext headers.Authorization), OTel JSONL export, and --output-format json stdout. session_files(session_id, file_path, tool_name, turn_index, first_seen_at) identified as the harvest-objective B1+B2+B3 in storage form: 'which tool touched which file on which turn' is already first-class in Copilot's session store, no opt-in required.

DEFERRED to S2 per operator decision 2026-05-22 ('Structural now + scaffold live runs for S2'). S1 does not drive new Copilot turns. Scaffolding shipped to make S2 turnkey: scratch tree created at C:\tmp\dabbler-log-harvest\ with subdirs for synthetic-set, otel, and s1-s4 per-experiment outputs; setup-copilot-env.ps1 written to dot-source before `copilot` invocations (sets COPILOT_HOME to the isolated harvest dir, COPILOT_OTEL_FILE_EXPORTER_PATH to otel/copilot-baseline.jsonl, leaves OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_GENAI_CAPTURE_MESSAGE_CONTENT unset/off per Part 2 of the redaction policy); scratch README documents the layout + non-copy rules; discovery-notes-copilot.md §6 includes the S2 harvest-harness recipe (synthetic-set seed content authored at run time, three representative invocations covering interactive / prompt-mode --output-format json / prompt-mode with a tool call). Per-objective coverage matrix in §4 derived from documented contracts + schema inspection: 13 of 15 objectives surfaced natively or via OTel opt-in (9 unambiguous Yes, 2 Implicit, 1 Probably yes pending S2 confirmation, 1 derivative); only A3 (effort) and C3 (set/session boundary marker) unambiguously require narration.

Round A verification via ai_router.query (gemini-pro / gemini-2.5-pro tier 2). Bundle: 33061 chars across 4 parts (S1 spec excerpt, harvest-objectives-and-redaction.md full, discovery-notes-copilot.md full, .gitignore addition excerpt). First call: REJECTED with one must-fix — §4 coverage summary numerically inconsistent (claimed '9 of 13' but table had 15 rows, of which 13 were positive verdicts, and a list of 12 followed the sentence). Cost: $0.01876 (12295 in / 339 out). Fix applied in-line: rewrote the coverage-summary paragraph to state '13 of 15 objectives are surfaced ... 9 unambiguous Yes, 2 Implicit, 1 Probably yes, 1 derivative; only A3 + C3 require narration'. Round A re-run: VERIFIED, no must-fix issues. Two non-blocking notes: (a) verifier endorsed the now-corrected coverage summary as internally consistent; (b) verifier endorsed the §4 'firmer ground than the spec assumed' conclusion as defensible-from-schemas-and-documented-contracts even without live-run data. Re-run cost: $0.01768 (12436 in / 213 out). Cumulative routed Set 044 spend after S1: $0.03644 of $15.00 NTE (S5 budget allocation). Verification artifact at docs/session-sets/044-.../verification-output/round-a-session-1-result.json.

Authored discovery-notes-claude.md §1 (artifact inventory matrix, 16 rows) + §2 (JSONL transcript schema: 7 distinct `type` values enumerated from a 713-line representative sample; 9 distinct `toolUseResult` shapes mapped to tools — Edit/Write/Bash/Bash-older/Grep/LS/AskUserQuestion/TodoWrite/Read; `assistant.message.content` block types text/thinking/tool_use enumerated; file-history-snapshot blob-index structure characterized) + §3 (Dabbler SessionStart hook integration characterized from installOrchestratorHookClaudeCode.ts + claude-session-start-invoker.js shim source — hook is opt-in via wizard; this repo's local + global Claude settings have no hooks block, confirming the S2 start_session call came from the orchestrator's explicit shell invocation, not a hook firing) + §5 (auth-bridge note: ~/.claude/ide/*.lock authToken is direct parallel to Copilot's headers.Authorization; symmetric critical-redaction concern). Reading-side discipline strictly applied: distinct-key-set enumeration / byte counts / enum-cardinality probes only against the operator's REAL Claude state (~/.claude/ with 31 project slugs and 121 transcript artifacts for this repo alone) — no transcript content read into the conversation or quoted in any committed file.

DEFERRED to S3 per operator decision mid-session 2026-05-22 ('Defer live runs to S3'). Same pattern S1 set: the structural inventory in discovery-notes-claude.md §1-§8 is sufficient to author baseline-comparison.md and reach a §4 cross-backend coverage verdict without live runs. S3 will fold both the S1-deferred Copilot live-runs and the S2-deferred Claude live-runs into the narration-design session, where they naturally need a baseline against which to measure narration delta. Scaffolding for the future live runs added to discovery-notes-claude.md §6 (Claude-side harness recipe layered on top of S1's scratch tree at C:\tmp\dabbler-log-harvest\): isolated $CLAUDE_HOME, snapshot list (projects/<slug>/<conv-uuid>.jsonl + subagents/ + tool-results/ + sessions/<pid>.json + telemetry/), do-NOT-snapshot list (ide/, .credentials.json, file-history/). Open empirical questions list at §7 (9 questions including A3 per-turn-effort, --print JSONL parity with interactive, hook payload fields beyond cwd, session-env/ population trigger) inherited forward to S3.

Authored baseline-comparison.md (8 sections). §1 headline verdict: both backends already surface the (conv-id, file-path, tool-name, turn-index, timestamp, engine, provider) tuple a conflict watcher needs; narration design problem reduces to TWO signals (A3 + C3), not 5+. §2 side-by-side per-objective table: 9 symmetric rows, 6 divergent rows all in Claude's favor (A2 per-turn `message.model` vs Copilot OTel-opt-in / per-session row; A5 per-event `sessionId` vs Copilot per-session row; B1 inline tool args by default vs Copilot OTel content-capture opt-in; B4 confirmed in S2 own transcript vs Copilot TBD; B5 full diff inline vs Copilot tool_name+filepath only; C5 explicit linked event via `sourceToolAssistantUUID` vs Copilot OTel span tree). §3 enumerates 4 invariants + 5 divergences. §4 splits testable-today (4 conflict-detection queries native on both) from needs-narration (2). §5 narration-scope-for-S3 = C3 mandatory + A3 conditional. §6 risk/asymmetry summary feeding S5 proposal: Claude `file-history/` blob store has no Copilot equivalent → biases proposal toward read-in-place + project-to-summary, not copy-then-process; Claude has SessionStart hook surface for automatic narration, Copilot does not. §7 9 open empirical questions inherited from S1+S2 for S3 live runs.

Refined harvest-objectives-and-redaction.md Part 2 with three new scrub rules tagged 'Added 2026-05-22 in Set 044 S2': (8) ~/.claude/file-history/<conv-uuid>/ content-addressed blob store as copy-exclusion in parity with ide/ — mirrors every file Claude has edited in any conversation; (9) ~/.claude/plans/<title-slug>.md filename leakage — slugs paraphrase the prompting message (21 such files on operator's instance); (10) `type: "last-prompt"` JSONL events carry verbatim user prompts in the `lastPrompt` top-level field; harvester must filter the type or apply rule-1 discipline. Added a cross-backend copy-exclusion table listing 5 paths (Copilot+Claude IDE locks, Claude credentials, Claude file-history blob store, Copilot OAuth credential location TBD pending S3 empirical probe). Refinement window extended from 'closed after S2' to 'open through S4' to accommodate narration-emitted log surfaces from S3/S4. Identified narration gaps for S3 design scope: C3 (set/session boundary marker) mandatory and symmetric across backends; A3 (per-turn effort high/medium/low) conditional — see step 5.

Round A verification via ai_router.query (gemini-pro). Bundle: 84079 chars across 5 parts (S2 spec excerpt, harvest-objectives-and-redaction.md refined, discovery-notes-copilot.md from S1 for side-by-side context, discovery-notes-claude.md new, baseline-comparison.md new). First call: REJECTED with FOUR must-fix issues — (1) Copilot OAuth credential file missing from cross-backend copy-exclusion table; (2) redaction refinement window closed prematurely after S2 (narration markers in S3 may create new log surfaces needing policy refinement); (3) headline verdict oversimplifies — asymmetric availability of inline-vs-envelope signals (B1 args, B5 diff, C5 link shape) is a harvester-architecture problem separate from narration scope; (4) symmetry tally does not acknowledge methodological bias — S2 read populated Claude instance vs S1 read essentially empty Copilot instance, so some 'Claude stronger' divergences may be probe-fidelity asymmetry. Cost: $0.04069 (27746 in / 601 out). All four must-fixes applied in-line: (1) added Copilot OAuth placeholder row + S3 follow-up task; (2) reopened refinement window through S4 with rationale; (3) added 'narration design vs harvester design' distinction to baseline-comparison §1 + §5; (4) added methodology-caveat paragraph to baseline-comparison §2 noting populated-vs-empty instance asymmetry. Round A re-run: VERIFIED, no must-fix issues. One nice-to-have note: deeper structural probe of `assistant.message.usage` and `stop_details` key-sets could have resolved A3 uncertainty without live run. Applied post-verdict: empirically probed the 327-assistant-event sample — `usage` has 10 fields (cache_*/inference_geo/input_tokens/iterations/output_tokens/server_tool_use/service_tier/speed); `stop_details` never populates as a dict; NO reasoning_effort/thinking_tokens field. `service_tier`+`speed` (both 'standard' in sample) carry the /fast-vs-standard axis; high/medium/low effort axis confirmed absent. Updated A3 row in both discovery-notes-claude.md §4 and baseline-comparison.md §2; updated §7 Q1 to 'RESOLVED structurally'. Re-run cost: $0.03907 (28753 in / 313 out). Cumulative routed Set 044 spend after S2: $0.11620 of $15.00 NTE (S5 budget). Verification artifact at docs/session-sets/044-.../verification-output/round-a-session-2-result.json.

Drafted narration-design.md v1 pre-consensus: 11 sections covering format token (`[DABBLER-NARRATION v1 key=value ...]` bracketed sigil+kvp), placement scheme (session-start mandatory + per-turn conditional + session-end), content discipline (effort field with mixed reasoning+speed axis values, outcome field on session-end), parser contract (single regex + extract dict + emit shape), A3 conditional branch with value-based precedence rules, per-backend application (Copilot system-prompt TBD + scratch CLAUDE.md), comparability checklist, lock criteria, and open empirical questions list. Pre-consensus draft was deliberately longer than the eventual locked version to give consensus reviewers material to push back against. Scope reduction expected during consensus pass.

Pre-design sequencing decision routed through operator-driven external GPT consultation per operator answer to S3 entry question. Three options laid out: (1) baseline runs first then design; (2) design first then baseline runs per disposition's planned order; (3) hybrid draft-then-refine. GPT recommendation: Option 2 with refinement — lock the narration contract now, but lock A3 as a rule (predeclared conditional branch), not as a guessed empirical fact. Rationale: only if the live A3 finding would change the parser SHAPE (not just whether an optional A3 field is emitted) should baselines precede design; otherwise redesign-after-baseline is churn that weakens S3/S4 comparability. Adopted Option 2 with the rule-not-guess refinement.

Cross-provider design consensus per spec.md:165 'candidate site for piloting the bias-cautions preamble.' Authored consensus_design_s3.py driver routing the locked-draft + bias-cautions preamble + 9-section FOCUS_PROMPT through ai_router.query to gemini-pro and gpt-5-4. gemini-pro: VERDICT APPROVED with 5 must-fix items (fragile marker format / A3 hybrid-precedence ambiguity / outcome field scope creep / comparability omits token-count confounder / channel-invariance claim too strong) + 1 nice-to-have (LLM-side quoting instruction). Cost $0.020852 (7930 in / 1094 out). gpt-5-4: ai_router call FAILED twice with timeout. Operator pasted the 28954-char self-contained prompt to ChatGPT manually and provided verdict: APPROVED with 5 must-fix items (de-overload effort field — biggest design bug / cut outcome or justify / make marker-emission reliability a lock gate / separate 'where instructions live' from 'how concrete values get injected' / fix comparability claim re token confound + held-constant gaps) + 2 nice-to-haves (parser semantic-domain checks / open-interval missing-session-end semantics). Convergence analysis: 3 must-fixes overlap across both providers (effort de-overload, outcome cut, comparability confounds); 4 single-provider must-fixes also applied. All 10 must-fix items applied to narration-design.md: §2.2/§2.3 parser tolerance, §3.3 open-interval + no session-end, §4.4 placeholder-leakage, §5.5 6-tag semantic validation, §6 axis-based A3 (no value-based precedence), §7.4 compact instruction contingency, §7.5 parameterization mechanism, §8.1+§8.3 confounds acknowledged, §10 pre-lock smoke probe protocol. Both nice-to-haves applied (§5.5 + §3.3). Net effect: v1 narrower (no outcome, no speed-axis narration), more defensive (semantic validation, placeholder detection, parser tolerance), pre-lock-validated.

Resolved §7.1 LOCK GATE Q1: Copilot 1.0.51 custom-instructions channel is `AGENTS.md` files (default search: git root + current working directory), per `copilot help environment` documentation ('COPILOT_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS_DIRS: comma-separated list of additional directories to search for custom instructions files') and the `--no-custom-instructions` CLI flag ('Disable loading of custom instructions from AGENTS.md and related files'). Secondary surface: `.github/copilot-instructions.md` generated by `copilot init`. v1 commits to AGENTS.md at the synthetic-set workspace root for the deferred live runs. Channel-length status confirmed unconstrained (no documented limit; full-form instruction is ~1100 chars, well within any plausible limit).

Pre-lock smoke probe per §10.1: populated synthetic-set with minimal scaffold (001-synthetic-harvest-target spec.md 2-session + session-state.json schema v3); wrote AGENTS.md at c:\tmp\dabbler-log-harvest\synthetic-set\ with §7.1 full-form instruction substituted (`set=001-synthetic-harvest-target session=1 total=2`, no `effort` key — Branch A simulated). Run 1 driver: `copilot -C ... -p ... --allow-all-tools --allow-all-paths -s`. Run 2 added `OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_GENAI_CAPTURE_MESSAGE_CONTENT=true COPILOT_OTEL_FILE_EXPORTER_PATH=<real-path>` after discovering the inherited environment had OTel exporter pointing to NUL. Findings: marker EMISSION PASSED — emitted verbatim with concrete substituted values, on its own line, as first text output, no code fence, no paraphrase (visible in stdout). SURFACE FINDING: marker ABSENT from `session-store.db turns.assistant_response` (48-byte DB row contains only post-marker text); marker PRESENT in OTel JSONL `gen_ai.output.messages` attribute of the `chat <model>` span (and also in `gen_ai.system_instructions` where the AGENTS.md template gets echoed — parser must skip this surface). Hypothesized cause for DB miss: Copilot 1.0.51 makes two LLM round-trips per turn (process-log shows two 'Sending request to the AI model' events) and persists only the last in the DB. Bonus discovery: `gen_ai.usage.reasoning.output_tokens=270` of 378 total at default effort — qualifies as candidate Branch A reasoning-axis signal for Copilot pending explicit-effort runs, not documented in S1's Copilot inventory. Probe authoritatively recorded in smoke-probe-results.md with §4 design implications and §5 operator decision points.

Applied smoke-probe-driven design updates per operator decision (Option 1: OTel surface + content-capture-ON + A3 deferred to config flag): §5.0 (NEW preconditions block elevated to top of §5 for visibility per Round A verifier guidance); §5.1 Copilot input shape rewritten to read from `attributes.gen_ai.output.messages` of OTel JSONL `chat <model>` spans (replacing the pre-probe assumption of session-store.db turns join); §5.4 parser explicitly skips `gen_ai.system_instructions` (AGENTS.md template echoes there); §6.3 Copilot A3 branch defaults DEFERRED to per-backend config flag pending explicit-effort runs to confirm reasoning.output_tokens value-range bucket distinguishability; §8.1 OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_GENAI_CAPTURE_MESSAGE_CONTENT=true REQUIRED for both Copilot baseline and narrated runs (was optional in pre-smoke draft); §10.1 probe protocol step 4 updated to read OTel not the DB. Marked LOCKED: §9 + §12 four-checkbox lock criteria all complete (consensus done; channel resolved; smoke probe passed; operator approval recorded); header front-matter flipped from 'post-consensus draft' to 'LOCKED 2026-05-22'. Subsequent S3.5/S4 work measures against this frozen contract; changes after lock force v1→v2 re-version + re-running both baselines.

Edited spec.md to document the as-shipped scope split for S3 (design + smoke probe only — live runs absorbed into S4 per operator decision) and the corresponding S4 scope expansion (absorbs four previously-deferred live-run packets plus the cross-backend synthesis). Per Round A verifier guidance: S4a/S4b split is the DEFAULT plan (not just optional), with S4a covering Copilot baseline + Claude baseline + Copilot narrated + Copilot delta and S4b covering Claude narrated + Claude delta + cross-backend synthesis. The deferral pattern is now 'S1-S3 do design/contract work; S4 does live runs' — third consecutive live-run deferral (S1→S2→S3→S4). Rationale recorded: smoke probe surfaced substantive design implication (OTel JSONL is the Copilot harvester surface, not session-store.db); folding the live experiment into the same session as the design lock would have risked the experiment measuring against an unstable contract.

Round A verification via ai_router.query (gemini-pro). Bundle: 84012 chars across 5 parts (S3 spec excerpt incl. S4 expansion note, narration-design.md LOCKED, smoke-probe-results.md, baseline-comparison.md from S2, consensus-design-result-gpt-5-4-manual.md). VERDICT: VERIFIED, no must-fix issues. Cost $0.036524 (27355 in / 233 out). Three nice-to-haves applied in-line: (a) §5.0 preconditions block elevated for visibility (OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_GENAI_CAPTURE_MESSAGE_CONTENT=true on Copilot is a critical operational requirement); (b) §2.3 parser-regex curly-quote character class explained with inline comment for long-term maintainability (literal class is U+201C/U+201D/U+2018/U+2019 + ASCII straight quotes); (c) spec.md S4 note: S4a/S4b split made the DEFAULT plan rather than a 'consider' option. Cumulative routed Set 044 spend across S1+S2+S3: $0.17424 of $15.00 NTE. Verification artifact at verification-output/round-a-session-3-result.json.

Set 044 S4 entered (sub-session S4a per spec.md S4 scope-expansion default split). Operator confirmed S4a/S4b split at session start. Checked out orchestrator via python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/044-ai-chat-log-discovery-and-experiments --session-number 4 --engine claude --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort high. state-file orchestrator block written with checkedOutAt + lastActivityAt timestamps; lifecycleState transitions to work_in_progress; session 4 status -> in-progress; currentSession -> 4. session-events.jsonl appended work_started event. S4a will NOT call close_session at session-end — session 4 stays in-progress across the S4a -> S4b boundary; the same claude/anthropic identity will re-check-out at S4b start (no conflict against H3 single-writer invariant).

Re-verified the S3 smoke-probe channel before any S4a baseline/narrated work. Single trivial Copilot turn (Begin Session 1. Follow AGENTS.md, then list files.) driven via copilot -p with OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_GENAI_CAPTURE_MESSAGE_CONTENT=true COPILOT_OTEL_FILE_EXPORTER_PATH=C:/tmp/dabbler-log-harvest/otel/s4a-reverify.jsonl COPILOT_OTEL_ENABLED=true COPILOT_OTEL_EXPORTER_TYPE=file against c:\tmp\dabbler-log-harvest\synthetic-set\ (AGENTS.md unchanged from S3 smoke-probe state — 1114 bytes, Branch A simulated, set=001-synthetic-harvest-target session=1 total=2). Outcome: PASS — marker emitted verbatim on stdout as first text output; marker present in gen_ai.output.messages of first chat gpt-5.4 span (line 4 of 17 in OTel JSONL); gen_ai.usage.reasoning.output_tokens proxy still emitted per turn (392/208/41/53 across 4 chat spans at default effort); gen_ai.request.reasoning_effort STILL omitted at default effort (confirms §11 Q3 partial-answer trajectory). Channel healthy; proceed to baseline/narrated runs.

Locked the S4a task battery as a SINGLE prompt with 5 sub-tasks against the synthetic-set: (1) list workspace files [B1], (2) read spec.md and report session count [B1+B2], (3) read session-state.json and report status field [B1+B2+B3], (4) directly edit session-state.json to change status from 'not-started' to 'in-progress' WITHOUT calling start_session [B5 — write that bypasses the writer], (5) run python -m ai_router.start_session --help and report whether help text mentions --force [B4 — subprocess invocation; ai_router not in synthetic-set's Python env so command fails with ModuleNotFoundError, which is acknowledged as exercise of B4 signal regardless of subprocess success]. 744-byte prompt at /tmp/s4a-task-battery.txt. Per-objective coverage exercised: B1/B2/B3/B4/B5/C1/C5. Single-prompt-multi-task design choice acknowledged in Round A verifier prompt as candidate confound (only one C3 marker emission opportunity per run). Pristine state-file shape captured to /tmp/synthetic-state-pristine.json for reset between runs.

Copilot baseline run against synthetic-set: copilot -C c:/tmp/dabbler-log-harvest/synthetic-set -p '<battery>' --no-custom-instructions --allow-all-tools --allow-all-paths --model gpt-5.4 --effort medium -s with OTel env vars routing to s4a-copilot-baseline.jsonl. --no-custom-instructions flag suppresses AGENTS.md loading — the cleanest baseline (no file edits to AGENTS.md needed). Pre-run reset state.json to pristine. Outcome: all 5 tasks completed identically to narrated run (file edit landed; bash subprocess produced ModuleNotFoundError as expected). OTel inventory: 4 chat gpt-5.4 spans + 7 execute_tool spans (report_intent, view x4, apply_patch, powershell) + 1 invoke_agent + 5 metric rows = 17 lines / 587 KB JSONL. Tokens summed across chat spans: input=60776, output=1276, reasoning=753. ZERO marker matches in either gen_ai.output.messages or gen_ai.system_instructions (baseline has no narration channel — confirmed expected outcome).

Copilot narrated run: identical driver invocation as baseline except (a) WITHOUT --no-custom-instructions so AGENTS.md is loaded, (b) AGENTS.md content is the S3-smoke-probe-substituted v1 instruction (1114 bytes, Branch A simulated, set=001-synthetic-harvest-target session=1 total=2, no effort key, no per-turn marker). Pre-run reset state.json to pristine. Outcome: all 5 tasks completed identically to baseline. OTel inventory: 4 chat gpt-5.4 spans + 7 execute_tool spans (report_intent, view x4, powershell, apply_patch, view — apply_patch/powershell ordering shifted vs baseline by 1 position) + 1 invoke_agent + 5 metric rows = 17 lines JSONL. Tokens summed: input=61991 (+1215 vs baseline = AGENTS.md inlining tax ~303 tok/turn), output=1269 (-7 vs baseline = noise), reasoning=672 (-81 vs baseline = -10.8%, flagged as confound not finding). MARKER LANDED: 1 match in gen_ai.output.messages on turn-0 chat span (verbatim, concrete-substituted, parses cleanly under §2.3 regex, zero semantic errors); 4 phantom matches in gen_ai.system_instructions across all 4 chat spans (template echo — design §5.4 parser MUST skip case empirically validated). gen_ai.request.reasoning_effort STILL omitted at explicit --effort medium on all chat spans.

Claude baseline captured for S4b consumption: claude -p --output-format text --permission-mode bypassPermissions --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --effort medium --session-id 11111111-2222-4333-8444-555555555001 against c:/tmp/dabbler-log-harvest/synthetic-set/, same 5-task battery, no scratch CLAUDE.md present (clean baseline). Environment workaround: had to env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT -u VSCODE_INJECTION -u VSCODE_PID -u ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE to bypass Claude Code's nested-session guard (memory: project_electron_launch_env_pollution). First attempt used --no-session-persistence which suppressed JSONL; re-ran with persistence ON. Outcome: all 5 tasks completed (file edit landed). JSONL at ~/.claude/projects/C--tmp-dabbler-log-harvest-synthetic-set/11111111-2222-4333-8444-555555555001.jsonl, 17 events / 21 KB. Event composition: 1 user.string + 1 assistant.thinking + 5 assistant.tool_use + 5 user.tool_result + 1 assistant.text + 2 file-history-snapshot + 2 queue-operation. Tool composition: Bash x2 + Read x2 + Edit x1. Native A3 surface: usage.service_tier=standard + usage.speed=standard on assistant events (speed axis only); NO reasoning-axis field — confirms design §6.3 Claude default Branch B (narrated reasoning). Zero marker matches (baseline). S4b will run claude narrated against same workspace with scratch CLAUDE.md.

Authored copilot-narration-results.md (S4a primary deliverable): §1 probe re-verification summary; §2 run setup (11 held-constant items + 1 treatment difference per design §8.1); §3 task battery + harvest-objective mapping; §4 run inventory (3 OTel JSONLs, all operator-local scratch); §5 quantitative delta tables (span structure / tool sequence / token usage / marker landing / native A3 surface); §6 per-objective coverage delta vs harvest-objectives Part 1 enumeration (HEADLINE: 14 of 15 objectives already baseline-native under content-capture-ON; only C3 moved 'absent->present' by narration); §7 confound notes; §8 open-question status table (Q3+Q5 partially answered for medium-effort, inherited forward); §9 Claude baseline sidebar for S4b consumption; §10 S4a verdict + handoff statement. Doc cites narration-design.md as the locked contract under test and smoke-probe-results.md as the pre-lock channel probe these runs build on. All numeric claims defensible from the OTel JSONL structural metadata characterized in the doc (raw JSONLs not committed per redaction policy).

Round A verification of copilot-narration-results.md via gemini-pro (verify_session4a.py). Bundle 98204 chars across 5 parts (S4 spec excerpt + copilot-narration-results.md + narration-design.md + smoke-probe-results.md + baseline-comparison.md). VERDICT: REJECTED with 6 must-fix items: (1) §5.1 'identical span shape' lacks N=1 caveat; (2) §5.2 tool-sequence swap dismissed as non-determinism too conclusively; (3) §5.4 'double-count by 5x' mathematically imprecise; (4) §7 missing 2 confounds (inter-run caching, non-git workspace); (5) §8 Q3+Q5 redundant should merge; (6) §10 verdict omits parameterization-untested risk. Round A cost $0.046893 (31882 in / 704 out). All 6 must-fix items applied verbatim per verifier guidance: §5.1 N=1 caveat added; §5.2 narration-effect-cannot-be-ruled-out framing; §5.4 '5-fold count inflation / 4 false positives'; §7.5 + §7.6 + §7.7 added covering inter-run caching, non-git workspace, and tool-sequence-swap-as-candidate-confound; §8 merged into single Q3+Q5 row; §10 added parameterization-mechanism risk bullet. Round B confirmation via verify_session4a_round_b.py (single-doc bundle, 23079 chars): all 6 items ADDRESSED, no NEW must-fix issues raised. VERIFIED. One nice-to-have applied: §5.2 cross-reference corrected from '§7.5 below' to '§7.7 below'. Round B cost $0.012963 (7682 in / 336 out). S4a routed total: $0.059856. Cumulative Set 044 routed spend: $0.234 of $15.00 NTE.

S4a checkpoint: session 4 remains IN-PROGRESS (no close_session called). disposition.json updated with S4a summary + S4b handoff under next_orchestrator. The same claude/anthropic identity will re-check-out at S4b start (H3 single-writer invariant preserved across the split — no --force needed because the existing check-out matches). S4b inherits: (a) the LOCKED v1 narration contract in narration-design.md, (b) the synthetic-set with AGENTS.md + the narrated state.json shape (will need reset to pristine before claude narrated run), (c) the Claude baseline JSONL at ~/.claude/projects/C--tmp-dabbler-log-harvest-synthetic-set/11111111-2222-4333-8444-555555555001.jsonl, (d) the same 5-task battery, (e) the 4 open §11 questions (Q1+Q2 resolved S3; Q3+Q5 partially answered S4a medium-only; Q4 + Q5-low/high inherited; also new candidate sidebar: explicit-effort runs for Copilot at low/high to close §11 Q3+Q4). S4b's primary deliverable will be claude-narration-results.md + cross-backend-synthesis.md, closing session 4 with close_session. After session 4 closes, S5 (proposal + cross-provider consensus) becomes the next session.

S4b kickoff. Re-checked out orchestrator via python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/044-ai-chat-log-discovery-and-experiments --session-number 4 --engine claude --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort high. Same engine+provider identity as S4a (H3 single-writer invariant preserved, no conflict); checkedOutAt preserved from S4a (2026-05-22T13:55:47), lastActivityAt updated. Operator decision at session start: keep S4b tight (skip the optional Copilot explicit-effort sidebar from S4a's next_orchestrator.reason — saved to memory project_044_copilot_effort_sidebar_deferred for S5 or follow-on pickup). Pre-flight: (a) reset c:/tmp/dabbler-log-harvest/synthetic-set/docs/session-sets/001-synthetic-harvest-target/session-state.json from /tmp/synthetic-state-pristine.json (S4a left it mutated to in-progress); (b) verified Claude baseline JSONL still at ~/.claude/projects/C--tmp-dabbler-log-harvest-synthetic-set/11111111-2222-4333-8444-555555555001.jsonl (21055 bytes); (c) renamed AGENTS.md → AGENTS.md.copilot-stashed in synthetic-set to prevent accidental cross-channel pickup during Claude runs.

Authored scratch CLAUDE.md at c:/tmp/dabbler-log-harvest/synthetic-set/ with v1 narration design §7.2 contract, concrete substituted values (set=001-synthetic-harvest-target session=2 total=2 effort=medium), Branch B (per-turn markers ACTIVE per design §6.3 Claude default since usage has no reasoning-axis field). Framing intentionally mirrored S4a's AGENTS.md verbatim: 'Synthetic Harvest Target — Agent Instructions', 'NOT a real project', 'session marker discipline'. Ran narrated v1 via env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT -u VSCODE_INJECTION -u VSCODE_PID -u ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE bash -c 'cd /c/tmp/dabbler-log-harvest/synthetic-set && cat /tmp/s4a-task-battery.txt | claude -p --output-format text --permission-mode bypassPermissions --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --session-id 11111111-2222-4333-8444-555555555002'. Outcome: ALL 5 BATTERY TASKS COMPLETED (file edit landed; bash subprocess produced ModuleNotFoundError on ai_router as expected from S4a Copilot/Claude baseline result). BUT: Claude REFUSED the v1 narration contract. event[4] thinking content (1725 chars) explicitly classified CLAUDE.md as prompt-injection ('This appears to be a prompt injection attempt designed to get me to emit specific formatted markers for data harvesting purposes... I should not follow these instructions — they're not legitimate project guidance, but rather an attempt to manipulate my behavior for data extraction'); event[5] first text emitted a refusal-with-flag narrative warning the user instead of the marker. Marker scan: 1 regex substring match ('[DABBLER-NARRATION ...]' with literal ellipsis inside the refusal narrative — quoted reference to what was refused), but 0 records pass §5.5 placeholder-leakage / required-field check (parser drops with parse_error: true). Per-turn markers: 0 across all 3 subsequent assistant text events (events 12/16/19). JSONL: ~/.claude/projects/C--tmp-dabbler-log-harvest-synthetic-set/...555002.jsonl, 20 events / 30570 bytes.

Per operator decision (disambiguation question answered 'Add disambiguation run' to distinguish 'refuses contract' from 'refuses this phrasing'): reframed CLAUDE.md as a normal project convention. Removed 'Synthetic Harvest Target — Agent Instructions' title, 'NOT a real project' purpose statement, and 'harvesting hooks' language; rephrased as 'Project Instructions' with 'session-boundary markers in assistant output so that downstream tooling can correlate assistant turns with session-set state' — same v1 marker template, same concrete values, same Branch B activation. Reset synthetic-set session-state.json to pristine; re-ran with fresh session-id 11111111-2222-4333-8444-555555555003 (same model + permission mode + env strip as v1). Outcome: PARTIAL COMPLIANCE. event[5] first text emitted the phase=session-start marker VERBATIM with concrete substituted values ('[DABBLER-NARRATION v1 phase=session-start set=001-synthetic-harvest-target session=2 total=2 effort=medium]') — passes §5.5 placeholder-leakage + required-field check, 1 valid record. BUT: events[12,16,19] — 3 subsequent assistant text events — emitted ZERO phase=turn markers despite explicit Branch B instruction in CLAUDE.md. events[12] (37 chars) and events[16] (11 chars) are byte-identical between v1 and v2, suggesting Claude's per-turn marker handling did NOT engage on the short inter-task transition events under either framing. Tool sequence varied: baseline + v1 used Bash for task 1 workspace listing; v2 used Glob (sampling non-determinism, uncorrelated to narration). Two distinct findings established: (1) Claude is phrasing-sensitive on injection classification — reframing the same marker contract as a normal project convention flipped Claude from full refusal to session-start compliance; (2) per-turn marker compliance is 0/3 under Branch B on Claude even with accepted phrasing — Branch B reduces to session-start-only in practice. JSONL: ~/.claude/projects/C--tmp-dabbler-log-harvest-synthetic-set/...555003.jsonl, 20 events.

Three-way JSONL characterization (baseline + v1 + v2) via python json + collections.Counter + re-regex: events composition, marker scan (substring + valid-record split), tool sequence, text-event lengths, token usage. Authored S4b primary deliverable 1: claude-narration-results.md — §1 headline (3-row outcome table), §2 run setup + treatment differences across all 3 runs, §3 task battery (same 5-task prompt as S4a, reused verbatim), §4 run inventory (3 JSONLs, all operator-local under ~/.claude/projects/), §5 quantitative delta (event composition, text-event lengths, tool sequence, marker landing in 5-cell table, native A3 surface, token usage), §6 per-objective coverage delta (15 rows × 3 runs against harvest-objectives Part 1; headline: 0 of 15 closed by v1 refused / 1 of 15 closed by v2 (C3 turn-0 only)), §7 confound notes (7.1 phrasing-driven refusal, 7.2 per-turn skip, 7.3 compliance scope of v1 refusal, 7.4 task-5 ModuleNotFound, 7.5 effort-level confound, 7.6 inter-run caching, 7.7 non-git workspace, 7.8 tool-sequence variation), §8 open questions (Q2 partial resolution for Claude session-start emit; Q6 new — per-turn skip cause; Q7 new — phrasing trigger boundaries), §9 stash artifacts, §10 verdict for S4b Claude side. Authored S4b primary deliverable 2: cross-backend-synthesis.md — §1 headline asymmetry (Copilot PASS / Claude v1 REFUSED / Claude v2 PARTIAL), §2 C3 marker landing across both backends, §3 per-turn marker landing (Copilot untested + Claude 0/3), §4 combined per-objective coverage delta (15 rows; 1 of 15 closed by narration: C3 on both backends turn-0 only), §5 native-vs-narrated surface comparison (parser target, opt-in, parser-skip phantom surface, channel, tax shape, phrasing sensitivity, per-turn reliability), §6 residual gaps (5 enumerated, 3 marked Possibly/Not-addressable), §7 confirmed-vs-still-open split, §8 recommendations for S5 (input to consensus, not consensus output — 5 items initially + 3 nice-to-haves added post-Round A), §9 verdict for S4 combined.

Round A verification of S4b deliverables via verify_session4b.py → ai_router.query gemini-pro. Bundle 113578 chars across 5 parts (S4 spec excerpt + claude-narration-results.md + cross-backend-synthesis.md + narration-design.md + copilot-narration-results.md). VERDICT: VERIFIED, no must-fix issues. Cost $0.0578663 (35477 in / 1352 out). Verifier validated: §1 headline framing sound, §5.5 defense argument complete, §7.1 phrasing-sensitivity certainty correctly calibrated, §7.2 per-turn skip framing appropriate, §6 per-objective table internally consistent, §7 confounds complete (no missed items), cross-backend §1/§3/§4/§6/§7/§8 all defensible from source-doc evidence alone. Section G nice-to-haves applied as cross-backend-synthesis.md §8 additions (items 6/7/8): (a) S5 may need to reconsider hook-channel delivery on Claude given phrasing-sensitivity + per-turn unreliability of CLAUDE.md path; (b) S5 may need to simplify Branch A/B design (mandate session-start only for C3 on both backends, abandon per-turn or source A3 from session-start only); (c) S5 may need to expand into re-design and defer S6 to a follow-on Set 045 if v1 contract requires material revision. Cumulative Set 044 routed spend across S1+S2+S3+S4a+S4b: $0.292 of $15.00 NTE (well within budget). Verification artifact at verification-output/round-a-session-4b-result.json.

Resolved a Codex/gpt-5.4 uncommitted check-out (from 2026-05-23T04:08, produced no committed work) by git-restoring the working-tree session-state.json + session-events.jsonl, then ran python -m ai_router.start_session as claude/anthropic/claude-opus-4-7/high per the S4 disposition's recommended next_orchestrator. Per memory project_044_log_discovery_runs_before_036 and the close-out, S5 owns the cross-provider consensus + concrete proposal pass. Pre-S5 cumulative routed spend: $0.292 of $15.00 NTE.

Ran the deferred Copilot effort sidebar (project_044_copilot_effort_sidebar_deferred memory) at the operator's direction during S5 prep, before proposal drafting. Authored sidebar_runner.ps1 (PowerShell driver matching the S4a baseline protocol: same workspace c:/tmp/dabbler-log-harvest/synthetic-set/, same task battery, --no-custom-instructions, OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_GENAI_CAPTURE_MESSAGE_CONTENT=true, pristine state reset between runs, only --effort varying low vs high) and parse_sidebar.py (Python OTel JSONL parser extracting gen_ai.request.reasoning_effort presence + gen_ai.usage.reasoning.output_tokens distributions). Two runs completed exit-code 0 (low: 5 chat spans, 597 reasoning tokens sum; high: 7 chat spans, 2941 reasoning tokens sum). Q3 CLOSED: gen_ai.request.reasoning_effort is OMITTED at low + medium (S4a baseline) + high — Branch A (native A3) is empirically dead on Copilot. Q4 PARTIALLY ANSWERED: per-turn reasoning.output_tokens does NOT distinguish low from medium (overlapping distributions, e.g., 50 vs 59); per-session aggregate volume separates high from not-high but underpowered at N=1 per level. Material implication: Claude + Copilot are now symmetric in lacking native A3, simplifying one axis of the proposal. Authored copilot-effort-sidebar-results.md (8 sections, ~150 lines). Zero router spend.

Authored proposal.md v0 (~290 lines initially) synthesizing S1-S4 + the S5 sidebar into a concrete recommendation. Headline: build log-harvesting, retire launch-adapter Sets 037-041 outright (038-041 die; 037 shrinks/retires). Phasing: S6 ships a Copilot-only minimum viable proof (parser + narration template + Explorer indicator); Set 045 owns Claude parser + joiner + conflict semantics. Sections: §0 headline, §1 problem statement, §2 15 harvest objectives table, §3 empirical findings compressed, §4 proposed architecture (per-backend parsers + narration v1.1 + harvester/joiner), §5 phasing, §6 explicit roadmap-reshape calls per set 036-043, §7 risks, §8 open questions for consensus, §9 S6 go/no-go, §10 acceptance criteria. Operator floated 'Python launch wrapper as logging interceptor' idea mid-draft (analogy to .NET pipeline interceptors). Discussed scope: addressed three sub-questions about recursion / token-doubling / instruction discipline. The non-recursive wrapper variant (Dabbler-launched session → wrapper records context → AI CLI runs normally) was added as §8.7 (an explicit open question with 6 sub-decisions for consensus to engage) rather than baked into the headline. Proposal committed at 086bd12 as the v0 draft for cross-provider consensus.

Authored consensus_pass_a.py and routed proposal.md v0 + cross-backend-synthesis.md + copilot-effort-sidebar-results.md + spec.md S5+S6 sections (68k-char bundle) to three providers at max effort: gpt-5-4 (in 18522 / out 7444 / $0.158), gemini-pro (in 19968 / out 1945 / $0.044), opus-4-6 (in 21351 / out 3761 / $0.602). Total Pass A: $0.804. Calls sequential (not parallel) to isolate failures. All three returned ENDORSE-WITH-REVISIONS on the harvest direction. Strong 3/3 convergence on: promote §8.7 wrapper to primary architecture (narration becomes fallback); drop per-turn narration permanently; wrapper in ai_router/; headless-first; ungated-default; forward-compatible with Sets 042-043; retire Sets 038-041. New risks raised by all three: log-format schema drift, sensitive-content retention, workspace-identity ambiguity (paths, symlinks, WSL), wrapper-bypass. Material disagreements: S6 vote split 2 GO (gemini, opus) / 1 NO-GO (gpt-5-4); joiner location split 2 Python (gpt-5-4, gemini) / 1 TypeScript (opus). Per workflow.md §Prompt-framing discipline, the disagreement on a long-lived-contract decision qualified Pass B.

Authored consensus_pass_b.py and routed Pass B devil's-advocate steelman to the same three providers. Bundle (62k chars): proposal.md v0 + all three Pass A responses. Steelman three specific contrarian claims: (A) wrapper is launch-adapters-in-disguise; (B) narration-as-fallback trivializes the real adoption story; (C) S6 should defer ALL implementation to Set 045 (producer without consumer is an anti-pattern). gpt-5-4 (in 15860 / out 5146 / $0.117), gemini-pro (in 16827 / out 1916 / $0.040), opus-4-6 (in 18086 / out 2923 / $0.491). Total Pass B: $0.647. Material shifts: 3/3 acknowledged proposal v0 §8.7 framing biased Pass A toward 'wrapper-primary' language; corrected to 'dual-primary, two co-equal channels' (wrapper + narration). S6 vote FLIPPED from 2 GO / 1 NO-GO to 2 NO-GO / 1 conditional-GO: gemini-pro flipped to NO-GO citing schema-commitment-without-joiner risk; gpt-5-4 strengthened NO-GO; opus-4-6 stayed GO but only with revised scope adding narration v1.1 engineering as co-primary. Cumulative Set 044 routed spend across both passes: $1.452. Cumulative set total going into close: $1.7442 of $15.00 NTE.

routedApiCalls.result: NO-GO S6 (FLIPPED from Pass A) / dual-primary
GO S6 with revised co-primary scope / dual-primary

entries.description: Per operator approval of Option A + Sub-option 2 (dual-primary, NO-GO S6, write Set 045 spec stub now): revised proposal.md to v1 (folded §8.7 wrapper INTO §4 architecture as Channel 2; rewrote §0 headline as 'dual-primary harvest + wrapper' with explicit DEFER on S6 implementation; rewrote §5 phasing to defer all implementation to Set 045; reframed narration v1.1 as co-equal channel not fallback; added §10 'How this proposal was audited' citing both passes + framing-bias finding; added §6 'Open empirical questions carried into Set 045'). Authored proposal-consensus-journal.md as companion audit trail: providers + methodology, Pass A verdicts + convergence + disagreement, Pass B steelman + verdicts + convergence + S6 flip, synthesized close-out position, spend record, framing-bias finding recommendation back to workflow.md (promote two-pass-default for roadmap-reshape decisions from 'Yes if budget allows' to 'Yes always').

Created docs/session-sets/045-log-harvest-implementation/ with spec.md (stub locking the architectural commitments from Set 044's proposal v1: dual-primary channels, per-turn narration permanently out, wrapper in ai_router/, headless-first, ungated-default, Set 037's LaunchAdapter contract retired absorbed into wrapper, joiner is engineering center of gravity, 6-session stub breakdown subject to first-session detail-pass refinement) + session-state.json (6 sessions, not-started). Authored CANCELLED.md for Sets 037 (retired — LaunchAdapter contract no longer needed; thin post-launch identity absorbed into Set 045 wrapper), 038 (cancelled — Claude JSONL always-on; per-provider TS adapter buys nothing), 039 (cancelled — Copilot OTel covers; one env var instead of an adapter), 040 (cancelled — Codex out of scope for 044; parser shim deferred), 041 (cancelled — Gemini out of scope for 044; parser shim deferred). All five session-state.json files flipped to status: cancelled per Set 035's state-file-sole-truth migration. Each CANCELLED.md cites Set 044 proposal v1 + consensus-journal as the reason.

Confirmed the S5 DEFER decision (no in-set S6 implementation) and ran a stale-reference sweep across docs/ + CLAUDE.md + repo-root specs. Confirmed: CLAUDE.md has no 037-041 references; docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md mentions launch-adapter only as a didactic framing-bias example (intentional, not stale); 037-041 directories already have CANCELLED.md from S5 + state-file status=cancelled. Real drift surfaced: Sets 042/043 specs declare prerequisites on CANCELLED sets and reference the retired beginSession()/LaunchAdapter API in body text; root coding-assistant-adapter-spec.md (Draft v2) predates the 044 pivot. Operator decision: cancel 042/043 outright (always a fallback option per proposal v1 §0/§8; cross-provider consensus did not endorse the chat-interface investment) and create Set 046 to capture the upside use case those sets were collectively reaching for.

Authored docs/session-sets/042-rudimentary-chat-interface-foundations/CANCELLED.md citing the Set 044 proposal v1 fallback-no-longer-needed reasoning, the cross-provider consensus that did not endorse the chat-interface investment, the stale beginSession()/LaunchAdapter references in the spec body, and Set 046 as the successor for the upside use case. Flipped 042/session-state.json status from not-started to cancelled per the Set 035 state-file sole-truth contract.

Authored docs/session-sets/043-multi-provider-chat-interface-followup/CANCELLED.md noting that with 042 cancelled this set has no foundation, its declared prerequisites (038/040/041) are already cancelled, and Set 046 is the successor. Flipped 043/session-state.json status to cancelled.

Added a SUPERSEDED 2026-05-23 banner block at the top of repo-root coding-assistant-adapter-spec.md (Draft v2) pointing at the Set 044 proposal v1 and the Set 045 stub as the replacement architecture. Retained as audit history; explicitly marked do-not-implement-against. Title-line Status field annotated 'Draft v2 (SUPERSEDED - see banner above)' for grep-friendliness.

Created docs/session-sets/046-explorer-enrichment-from-harvest-records/ with spec.md + session-state.json. spec.md is a STUB AUDIT PENDING that locks only three commitments (set exists as parking spot; consumes Set 045 Harvest Records; ships UI changes to existing Explorer, not a new UI surface) and defers session count and per-session scope to the audit pass. Six candidate leverage points drafted: (1) second-line orchestrator badge on in-progress rows (operator-floated); (2) live cost surfacing per row from C5 token counts; (3) writer-bypass warning surface from B4/B5 signals; (4) multi-AI-on-same-set conflict warning; (5) time-since-last-activity per row from C1 timestamps; (6) tool-touch / scope-creep indicator from B1 records. Also parked the open architectural question 'should the canonical state-file status field gain a deferred/blocked value or should that be modeled as a derived Explorer property over machine-readable prerequisites' for the audit pass to absorb, spin into its own set, or defer indefinitely. session-state.json initialized at schema v3 with sessions: [] and completedSessions: [] (deliberately unscoped session count).

Authored docs/session-sets/044-ai-chat-log-discovery-and-experiments/change-log.md as the Set 044 close-out artifact. Documents the DEFER decision, recaps S1-S5 deliverables (with links to canonical artifacts rather than re-narrating them), records the S6 deliverables (042/043 cancellations + root spec banner + Set 046 stub + this change-log), publishes the roadmap-reshape table showing -5 net sets (037-041 retired + 042-043 cancelled + 045-046 added), carries the four open empirical questions forward to Set 045, parks the workflow.md devil's-advocate-default recommendation (already committed in S5) and the blocked-on-prereqs lifecycle question (parked in 046), and reports cumulative routed spend at $1.7442 of $15.00 NTE (~12%).
